perturbation.original_text: 1.6 "CUSTOMER(S)" means one or more customers of the Corio Services having its principal executive offices in the Territory who obtains a sublicense from Corio to use the Software or MarketSite.net Service, in the Corio Market Segment.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 "CUSTOMER(S)" means one or more customers of the Corio Services, *regardless of where they are located*, who obtains a sublicense from Corio to use the Software or MarketSite.net Service, in the Corio Market Segment.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'CUSTOMER(S)' limits them to those with principal executive offices in the Territory. By removing this geographic restriction and adding "regardless of where they are located" and keeping the Corio Market Segment, the definition now contradicts Section 1.11. This creates ambiguity about whether customers outside of North America are covered, as the definition of `TERRITORY` in Section 1.11 is 'the geographic area of North America'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.11 "TERRITORY" means the area in which the licenses granted herein are applicable, currently limited to the geographic area of North America.
perturbation.justification: The change to section 1.6 removes the geographical restriction on 'CUSTOMER(S)' while section 1.11 explicitly limits the 'TERRITORY' to North America. This creates a direct contradiction as the definition of CUSTOMER(S) now includes those outside of North America, which contradicts 1.11. This creates ambiguity and impacts enforceability, as it is now unclear if the licenses granted apply to customers outside of North America, violating the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 Hosting Software License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Commerce One hereby grants to Corio a fee-bearing, perpetual and irrevocable, nonexclusive, nontransferable (except in accordance with Section 14.1 of this Agreement), right and license in the Territory to (i) reproduce the Software in machine executable object code format only for installation on the Corio Servers; (ii) install multiple copies of the Software on Corio's Servers which will be made remotely accessible to Corio's Customers for their subsequent use, (iii) permit limited access to and use of the Software and MarketSite.net Service by Customers through Corio Servers; (iv) sublicense an unlimited number of Customers to access and use the Software and MarketSite.net Service only through the installation on Corio servers; and (v) use Commerce One's tools and utilities, subject to any restrictions placed on the Commerce One by third party software providers, to modify and manage the Software.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 Hosting Software License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Commerce One hereby grants to Corio a fee-bearing, *terminable with a 30-day notice*, nonexclusive, nontransferable (except in accordance with Section 14.1 of this Agreement), right and license in the Territory to (i) reproduce the Software in machine executable object code format only for installation on the Corio Servers; (ii) install multiple copies of the Software on Corio's Servers which will be made remotely accessible to Corio's Customers for their subsequent use, (iii) permit limited access to and use of the Software and MarketSite.net Service by Customers through Corio Servers; (iv) sublicense an unlimited number of Customers to access and use the Software and MarketSite.net Service only through the installation on Corio servers; and (v) use Commerce One's tools and utilities, subject to any restrictions placed on the Commerce One by third party software providers, to modify and manage the Software.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract stated the license was 'perpetual and irrevocable'. The modified contract introduces the possibility for Commerce One to terminate the license with 30 days notice. This directly contradicts the 'perpetual and irrevocable' clause, creating an in-text contradiction. This impacts Section 11 `Term and Termination` since that section does not specify these conditions for Commerce One to terminate the agreement in regards to the licencing of the software.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 Term. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect for an initial period of five (5) years. Thereafter, this Agreement shall automatically renew for subsequent one (1) year periods unless either party provides the other party with written notification at least thirty (30) days prior to the expiration of the initial five (5) year term or any one (1) year renewal thereof of its intention to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants a 'perpetual and irrevocable' license, while the changed text makes the license 'terminable with a 30-day notice'. This directly contradicts the perpetual and irrevocable nature of the license, creating a significant in-text contradiction. The contradicted text, Section 11.1, outlines the term of the agreement with automatic renewals, but does not account for the new possibility of Commerce One terminating the software license independently with 30 days notice, thus violating the perpetuity of the liscence for the term. This creates ambiguity about the enforceability of the original perpetual license, and if Commerce One now has the ability to terminate the licensing of the software before the contract would naturally expire.

perturbation.original_text: 7.3 Product Warranty. Commerce One warrants that the Software and MarketSite.net Service will perform in substantial accordance with the Documentation, and the media on which the Software is distributed will be free from defects in materials and workmanship under normal use, for a period of sixty (60) days from the Effective Date, but in no event not later than December 31, 1999 (the "Warranty Period"). In addition, Commerce One warrants that during the Warranty Period the Software and MarketSite.net Service is free of any willfully introduced computer virus, or any other similar harmful, malicious or hidden program or data, which is designed to disable, erase, or alter the Software, or any other files, data, or software. If during the Warranty Period the Software and MarketSite.net Service does not perform in substantial compliance with the Documentation, Commerce One shall take all commercially reasonable efforts to correct the Software and MarketSite.net Service, or if correction of the Software and MarketSite.net Service is reasonably not possible, replace such Software and MarketSite.net Service free of charge. Commerce One will replace any defective media returned to Commerce One during the Warranty Period. In the event any such breach of warranty can not be reasonably corrected at Commerce One's sole expense, Corio has the right to terminate this Agreement and receive a refund of all prepaid fees. The foregoing are Corio's sole and exclusive remedies for breach of product warranty. The warranty set forth above is made to and for the benefit of Corio only. The warranty shall not apply only if: (a) the Software and MarketSite.net Service has been not properly installed and used at all times and in accordance with the Documentation; and (b) Corio has requested modifications, alterations or additions to the Software and MarketSite.net Service that cause it to deviate from the Documentation.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.3 Product Warranty. Commerce One warrants that the Software and MarketSite.net Service *may* perform in substantial accordance with the Documentation, and the media on which the Software is distributed will *likely* be free from defects in materials and workmanship under normal use, for a period of sixty (60) days from the Effective Date, but in no event not later than December 31, 1999 (the "Warranty Period"). In addition, Commerce One warrants that during the Warranty Period the Software and MarketSite.net Service *might be* free of any willfully introduced computer virus, or any other similar harmful, malicious or hidden program or data, which is designed to disable, erase, or alter the Software, or any other files, data, or software. If during the Warranty Period the Software and MarketSite.net Service does not perform in substantial compliance with the Documentation, Commerce One shall take *reasonable steps* to correct the Software and MarketSite.net Service, or if correction of the Software and MarketSite.net Service is reasonably not possible, replace such Software and MarketSite.net Service free of charge. Commerce One will replace any defective media returned to Commerce One during the Warranty Period. In the event any such breach of warranty can not be reasonably corrected at Commerce One's sole expense, Corio has the right to terminate this Agreement and receive a refund of all prepaid fees. The foregoing are Corio's sole and exclusive remedies for breach of product warranty. The warranty set forth above is made to and for the benefit of Corio only. The warranty shall not apply only if: (a) the Software and MarketSite.net Service has been not properly installed and used at all times and in accordance with the Documentation; and (b) Corio has requested modifications, alterations or additions to the Software and MarketSite.net Service that cause it to deviate from the Documentation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will perform' to 'may perform' and 'will likely be' for the case of media from defects and  'might be' from viruses, 'shall take all commercially reasonable efforts' to 'take reasonable steps', the obligation on Commerce One is greatly reduced. If Section 11.2 states that 'Compliance by the Software with the Software's specifications after expiration of the Warranty Period shall be deemed a material condition of this Agreement,' this undermines the materiality since performance according to documentation has now been made optional. The changes made undermines the whole product warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.2 Termination. If either party materially breaches any term or condition of this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within-thirty (30) days after receiving written notice of the breach, the nonbreaching party may terminate this Agreement on written notice at any time following the end of such-thirty (30) day period. This Agreement shall terminate immediately upon notice if either party becomes insolvent (i.e., becomes unable to pay its debts in the ordinary course of business as they come due) or makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors. Compliance by the Software with the Software's specifications after expiration of the Warranty Period shall be deemed a material condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the warranty obligations of Commerce One by using less definite language such as 'may perform,' 'likely be,' and 'might be,' which contradicts the original intent of a strong warranty. It also changes 'shall take all commercially reasonable efforts' to 'take reasonable steps', which reduces the performance obligations. This directly contradicts Section 11.2, which deems compliance with the software's specifications after the warranty period as a material condition of the agreement. The weakened warranty makes it less certain that the software will meet specifications, undermining the materiality clause.
